-{"id":"bd-10pw","title":"[IDEA-04.1] Cursor state schema + state object mapping","description":"## Goal
Define the canonical cursor state schema and mapping to ConnectorStateObject.

## Details
- Specify cursor fields (offset, last_seen_id, watermark) as connector-specific CBOR payload.
- Define sequencing rules (monotonic seq, idempotent updates).
- Document how state chains are compacted via snapshots.

## Tests
- Golden vectors for cursor serialization.
- Validation tests for invalid regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cursor schema is stable and documented for connector authors.
- State chains detect regressions and fork scenarios.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:31.939207818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:31.946251144Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10pw","depends_on_id":"bd-2cqj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:31.946205710Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11g2","title":"E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling","description":"# E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling

## Goal
Define a single structured logging schema for E2E scripts and fcp-e2e harness output, plus a validator.

## Scope
- JSONL schema (fields: timestamp, script, step, correlation_id, duration_ms, result, artifacts, error_code, details).
- Validator tool or test that fails if logs are malformed.
- Documented guidelines for step logging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is documented under docs/testing/.
- Validator runs in CI or as part of e2e runner.
- All existing e2e scripts conform to the schema.
","notes":"Script E2E runners now enforce schema validation: added requirement for fcp-e2e and call to validate log JSONL at end of each scripts/e2e/*.sh. Updated denial_path to structured pass/fail logs with correlation_id/duration_ms/details; moved extra fields into details for revocation_flow and taint_approval.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:25:37.185809562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:15:39.540921391Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T21:15:39.540851772Z","close_reason":"Completed: unified schema/validator; scripts emit and validate JSONL","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11g2","depends_on_id":"bd-3p99","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:25:37.199406397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12ke","title":"[IDEA-23.1] Implement lifecycle state machine + canary policy","description":"## What
Implement lifecycle state tracking and canary rollout logic in `fcp-host`.

## How
- Add persistent lifecycle records (mesh object or host state).
- Health-based promotion/rollback thresholds.
- APIs for `promote`, `rollback`, and `status`.

## Success Criteria
- Canary failure triggers rollback with audit event.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Lifecycle state persists across restarts.
- State transitions are auditable and logged.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:45:38.739990117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:24:01.544185070Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:24:01.544115060Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12ke","depends_on_id":"bd-1zw6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:45:38.746780963Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
